analysis.overall_assessment: Your data shows excellent autonomic stability with strong HRV averaging 63.6ms and well-controlled resting HR at 47.4bpm, indicating good POTS management. However, consistently low deep sleep (averaging 1.2h, often below 1h) may be limiting immune recovery for Hashimoto's and vestibular compensation. The concerning May 4th crash (HRV dropped to 35, RHR spiked to 56, minimal sleep) requires immediate attention to prevent autonomic decompensation.
whats_going_well: Excellent autonomic control with HRV consistently above 55ms on most days (75ms on 4/28, 77ms on 5/1, 72ms on 5/2) indicating strong parasympathetic recovery
Resting heart rate remains stable in low 40s-40s range (44-49bpm) showing good cardiovascular fitness and absence of thyroid-induced tachycardia
Temperature deviations stable at 0.0°C average with no spikes suggesting well-controlled Hashimoto's inflammation and effective supplement protocol
areas_to_improve: Deep sleep critically insufficient - only 0.6-0.8h on 4/28, 5/1, 5/2 when optimal is 1.5-2h for immune repair; consider earlier magnesium glycinate timing or sleep environment optimization
May 4th represents concerning autonomic crash with 44% HRV drop (35 vs 72 previous day) and 18% RHR increase - identify triggers from May 3rd activities
Activity pacing needs refinement - high step days (8365 on 5/3) preceded the autonomic crash, suggesting overexertion threshold around 8000+ steps for current capacity
analysis.autonomic_assessment: HRV trends show excellent baseline autonomic function averaging 63.6ms with most days above 55ms, indicating strong parasympathetic recovery and well-managed POTS. However, the dramatic May 4th crash (HRV 35, RHR 56) following high activity suggests you're operating near your autonomic ceiling. The pattern shows good day-to-day stability until overexertion triggers sympathetic overdrive.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Zero temperature deviation and stable metrics suggest well-controlled Hashimoto's with no current flare activity. However, consistently low deep sleep (0.6-1.1h vs optimal 1.5-2h) may be limiting overnight immune system repair and could predispose to future autoimmune activation.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (77-89) and stable HRV on most days indicate good capacity for vestibular rehab exercises. However, May 4th shows complete vestibular rehab contraindication with severely compromised recovery metrics requiring rest day.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize deep sleep optimization through earlier magnesium timing, cooler sleep environment, or blue light restriction 2h before bed - your 0.6-0.8h deep sleep is severely limiting immune recovery for Hashimoto's and may have contributed to the May 4th autonomic crash.
